en.name: Chimp Test
en.desc: Can you beat a chimp at memory?
en.description: The Chimp Test is based on a famous scientific study that showed chimpanzees outperforming humans at certain memory tasks! Numbers flash briefly on screen in random positions, then disappear. Your task: recall and click the positions in numerical order (1, 2, 3...). Research shows chimps can handle sequences of 9+ numbers with remarkable accuracy. Can you match their memory power?
en.objectives: Remember the positions of numbered squares after they briefly appear, then click them in order from 1 to 9 (and beyond). Progress through increasingly difficult levels with more numbers to remember. Test your working memory against our primate cousins!
en.howToPlay: Numbers flash briefly on screen. After they disappear, click the squares in order from 1 to 9. Get them all right to advance to higher levels with more numbers.

rules.controls: Watch the numbered squares flash on screen. After they disappear, click the empty squares in numerical order (1, 2, 3...). Complete the sequence correctly to advance.
mechanics: Numbers 1-9 appear briefly in random positions
Click squares in correct numerical order
One mistake ends the current attempt
Levels increase difficulty (more numbers, shorter display)
features: Visual Memory Challenge - Test spatial recall
Progressive Difficulty - More numbers as you advance
Score Tracking - Compare with chimp performance
Instant Feedback - Know immediately if correct

tips: Use chunking - group numbers into patterns
Stay calm and focused during the flash
Practice to improve your working memory capacity

faq.question: Why is it called the Chimp Test?
faq.answer: This test replicates a famous study where chimpanzees, particularly a chimp named Ayumu, could remember 9-number sequences with 90% accuracy, outperforming human adults!

faq.question: What's a good score?
faq.answer: Most humans struggle beyond 7 numbers. Reaching level 9 is impressive. Young chimps in studies regularly achieve 80-90% accuracy at level 9.

faq.question: How can I improve?
faq.answer: Practice regularly, use visualization techniques, and try chunking numbers into groups. Stay relaxed—stress hurts working memory performance.

faq.question: Why are chimps better at this?
faq.answer: Scientists believe chimps have superior eidetic (photographic) memory for brief displays. Young chimps especially excel at this specific task, though humans can improve with practice.

zh.name: 黑猩猩测试
zh.desc: 你能在记忆上打败黑猩猩吗？
zh.description: 黑猩猩测试基于一项著名的科学研究，显示黑猩猩在某些记忆任务上胜过人类！数字在屏幕上随机位置短暂闪现，然后消失。你的任务：回忆并按数字顺序（1、2、3...）点击位置。研究表明黑猩猩可以以惊人的准确度处理9+个数字的序列。你能匹配它们的记忆力吗？
zh.objectives: 在数字短暂出现后记住编号方格的位置，然后按1到9（及更多）的顺序点击它们。在越来越困难的级别中进步，需要记住更多数字。测试你的工作记忆对抗我们的灵长类表亲！
zh.howToPlay: 数字在屏幕上短暂闪现。消失后，按1到9的顺序点击方格。全部正确即可进入更高级别，有更多数字需要记忆。

rules.controls: 观看编号方格在屏幕上闪现。消失后，按数字顺序（1、2、3...）点击空白方格。正确完成序列即可前进。
mechanics: 数字1-9在随机位置短暂出现
按正确数字顺序点击方格
一次错误结束当前尝试
级别增加难度（更多数字，更短显示时间）
features: 视觉记忆挑战 - 测试空间回忆
渐进难度 - 随着进步数字更多
分数追踪 - 与黑猩猩表现比较
即时反馈 - 立即知道是否正确

tips: 使用分块 - 将数字分组成模式
将位置与空间记忆关联
在闪现时保持冷静专注
练习以提高你的工作记忆容量

faq.question: 为什么叫黑猩猩测试？
faq.answer: 这个测试复制了一项著名研究，其中黑猩猩，特别是一只名叫Ayumu的黑猩猩，能以90%的准确率记住9个数字的序列，胜过成年人类！

faq.question: 什么是好的分数？
faq.answer: 大多数人在7个数字以上就困难了。达到9级令人印象深刻。研究中的年轻黑猩猩在9级经常达到80-90%的准确率。

faq.question: 如何提高？
faq.answer: 定期练习，使用可视化技巧，尝试将数字分成组。保持放松——压力会损害工作记忆表现。

faq.question: 为什么黑猩猩更擅长这个？
faq.answer: 科学家认为黑猩猩对短暂显示有更优越的照相式记忆。年轻的黑猩猩尤其擅长这个特定任务，尽管人类可以通过练习提高。
